content_en:
QADAR (PREDESTINATION) We have explained above that the belief in qadar (predestination) brought by Islam is free from the weakness, laziness and apathy that have affected a large part of the Muslim ummah down through the ages in the name of belief in qadar. The cause of that has been the deviation of the Muslims from a proper understanding of the concept of qadar (predestination). Whoever ponders the belief in qadar (predestination) as brought by Islam will find that it has many great benefits which led and still lead to the reform of the individual and of the ummah. We will describe below some of these benefits of belief m predestination (qadar) that we have learned from this study: 1 Belief in Qadar (Predestination) is a Means - of Ridding Oneself of Shirk Many philosophers have claimed that good comes from Allah, the All-Glorious, and that evil is the creation of other gods; they said this in order to avoid attributing evil to Allah. 1 The Magians (Zoroastrians) claimed that Light was the creator of good, and Darkness was the creator of evil. Those among this ummah who claimed that Allah does not create the deeds of His slaves, or that He does not create the misguided deeds, are claiming that there are creators other than Allah. 1 Shifaa ' al- 'Alee/, p. 1 4.

Perfect Taw�eed (belief in the Oneness of the only God - Allah) is not attained except by those who affrrm that Allah Alone is the Creator of everything in the universe, and that His will prevails over His creation - what He wills happens and what He does not will does not happen. All of those who do not believe in qadar do not believe truly that their Lord is One and Alone ( Taw�eed) and do not acknowledge Him properly. Belief in predestination (qadar) is what marks the difference between Taw�eed and shirk. The one who believes in qadar affrrms that this universe and everything in it was created by One God; those who do not believe this imply that there are other gods and lords besides Allah. 2 Adhering to the True Path During Both - Times of E ase and Times of Difficulty Because of their shortcomings and weaknesses, people cannot always adhere to the straight path. Except those who are devoted to $alaah [prayers].� (Qur 'an 70: 19-22) Belief in qadar makes a person persist in his adherence to the straight path, so that he is not reckless at times of ease, and does not fall into despair when calamity strikes, for he knows that everything good that happens to him comes from Allah, and is not because of his intelligence or good planning: �And whatever of blessings and good things you have, it is from Allah .. ) (Qur 'an 1 6: 53) He is not like Qaaroon (Korah) who showed off before his people and arrogantly displayed before them the treasures and wealth that Allah had bestowed upon him. �Verily, Qaaroon [Korah] was of Moosa's [Moses] people, but he behaved arrogantly towards them. And We gave him of the treasures, that of which the keys would have been a burden to a body of strong men. Remember when his people said to him: 'Do not exult [with riches, being ungrateful to Allah].

Verily, Allah likes not those who exult [with riches, being ungrateful to Allah]. 'But seek, with that [wealth] which Allah has bestowed on you, the home of the Hereafter, and forget not your portion of lawful enjoyment in this world; and do good as Allah has been good to you, and seek not mischief in the land. Verily, Allah likes not the Mufsidoon [those who commit great crimes and sins, oppressors, tyrants, mischief-makers, corrupters]. ' He said: 'This has been given to me only because of the knowledge I possess . . .' � (Qur 'an 28: 7678) And when trials and tribulations befall a person, he knows that this is happening by the decree of Allah, as a test from Him, so he does not panic or despair, rather he seeks the reward of Allah and bears it with patience, and this faith brings contentment and tranquillity to the heart of the believer. .- � �I -� ·q ']" .:.-j\ 0 � � .::,\j \:; :i.._"!' � � � i.:f'· ..J � ,. • ,,, ·. · • i.r- .J' / � � . - / • � ,,,. ,,,, ,,,, ;'" i•"t .!('-:;� � � r-- '.r' ill - tfAA, J> ,, ...ti u- %?' -&.. / !fa. uj r..!;-"f' i;: �S ii� t.r·� / I� / . � @ . . . �b�\ � � );:� ']_; �No calamity befalls on the earth or in yourselves but it is inscribed in the Book of Decrees [Al-Lau/; al-Ma/Jfoo!?] before We bring it into existence. Verily, that is easy for Allah.

In order that you may not grieve at the things over that you fail to get, nor rejoice over that which has been given to you . . . � (Qur 'an 5 7: 22-23) And Allah praised His slaves, IJ � cl.:tl� � � ) ( ,J 1� ��,IJ � u�_; ( ,,.Ji,, �,j,. r�� � 'J" � . A� � >-:.",,..� 1�1 0:'.,, " � Jr :i.."f' • ... • , � e �_,.i::..�j' � �ji) L.;.;.; �1 � �� 1�!1' �Who, when afflicted with calamity, say: 'Truly, to Allah we belong and truly, to Him we shall return. ' They are those on whom are the Salawaat [i.e. who are blessed and will be forgiven] from their Lord, and [they are those who] receive His Mercy, and it is they who are the guided ones) (Qur 'an 2: 1 56-157) 3 The One Who Believes in Qadar - is Always Careful Those who believe in qadar are always careful, for: � $ 0�1 r_;ii1 -Ii �' � t;:t � . � . . � . . . None feels secure from the Plan of Allah except the people who are the losers) (Qur 'an 7: 99). People's hearts are always turning over and changing, for their hearts are between two of the fingers of A r-Ra/J,maan (Allah, the All­ Merciful), Who turns them over as He wills, and the trials which are sent against people's hearts are many.

The believer is always careful lest there come to him something which will lead him astray, just as he always fears that his final actions may be bad. This does not make him lazy and apathetic, rather it motivates him to strive persistently to follow the straight path, to do many righteous deeds and to avoid sins and punishable deeds. And the believer's heart is always attached to its Creator, calling upon Him, putting its hope in Him, seeking His help and asking Him to make it adhere fmnJy to the straight path, and to be guided aright. 4 Facing Difficulties and D angers - with a Strong Heart When a person believes that everything that happens is decreed, and that provision and lifespans are in the hand of Allah, then he can confront difficulties and ordeals with a strong heart and with his head held high. This belief was one of the greatest motives which spurred the mujaahideen (warriors in the way of Allah) to acts of courage on the battlefield without feeling scared. They would seek death wherever they could find it, throwing themselves into dangerous situations where they thought they would be killed.

Then you would find one of them later on, dying in his own bed (of natural causes); he would weep for not having fallen in battle as a shaheed (martyr) even though he exposed himself to dangers. This belief is one of the greatest sources of strength for the righteous in confronting evildoers and tyrants. For the sake of Allah, they do not fear the blame of any man, because they know that the matter is in the hand ofAllah, and whatever He decreed for them would come to them. They were not afraid to speak the word of truth lest their provision be cut off, for provision is in the hand of Allah. Whatever provision Allah has decreed for His slave cannot be withheld from anyone, and whatever Allah withholds from His slave, none can cause it to reach him.

content_id:
Kita telah menjelaskan di atas bahwa keyakinan tentang qadar (takdir) yang dibawa oleh Islam bebas dari kelemahan, kemalasan, dan apatis yang telah mempengaruhi sebagian besar umat Muslim sepanjang zaman atas nama keyakinan kepada qadar. Penyebab dari itu adalah penyimpangan kaum Muslim dari pemahaman yang benar tentang konsep qadar. Barangsiapa merenungkan...

Dr. Al-Asyqar menjelaskan secara terperinci manfaat-manfaat beriman kepada qadar yang benar:

Manfaat 1: Ketenangan hati — Seorang yang benar-benar beriman kepada qadar mengetahui bahwa segala yang menimpanya adalah ketetapan Allah yang Maha Bijaksana dan Maha Pengasih. Maka hatinya tenang menghadapi cobaan, dan ia tidak terguncang oleh musibah. Allah berfirman: "Tidak ada suatu musibah pun yang menimpa seseorang kecuali dengan izin Allah. Dan barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah, niscaya Dia akan memberi petunjuk kepada hatinya." (QS. At-Taghabun: 11)

Manfaat 2: Kebebasan dari ketakutan yang berlebihan — Orang yang beriman kepada qadar mengetahui bahwa ajal, rezeki, dan nasibnya semua ada di tangan Allah. Maka ia tidak takut secara berlebihan kepada makhluk, tidak rendah diri kepada penguasa zalim, dan tidak cemas berlebihan tentang masa depannya.

Manfaat 3: Dorongan untuk beramal — Iman kepada qadar yang benar mendorong manusia untuk beramal sebaik mungkin, karena ia mengetahui bahwa mengambil sebab-sebab adalah bagian dari qadar itu sendiri. Ia tidak bermalas-malasan dengan alasan "sudah takdir" — karena ini adalah pemahaman yang salah tentang qadar.
content_ar:
القدر (القضاء والقدر) We have explained above that the الاعتقاد in القدر (القضاء والقدر) brought by الإسلام is free from the الضعف, laziness and apathy that have affected a large part of the مسلم الأمة down through the ages in the name of الاعتقاد in القدر. The cause of that has been the deviation of the المسلمون from a proper الفهم of the concept of القدر (القضاء والقدر). Whoever ponders the الاعتقاد in القدر (القضاء والقدر) as brought by الإسلام will find that it has many great benefits which led and still lead to the reform of the individual and of the الأمة. We will describe below some of these benefits of الاعتقاد m القضاء والقدر (القدر) that we have learned from this study: 1 الاعتقاد in القدر (القضاء والقدر) is a Means - of Ridding Oneself of الشرك Many philosophers have claimed that الخير comes from الله, the All-Glorious, and that الشر is the creation of other gods; قالوا this من أجل avoid attributing الشر to الله. 1 The Magians (Zoroastrians) claimed that Light was the creator of الخير, and Darkness was the creator of الشر. Those among this الأمة who claimed that الله does not create the deeds of His slaves, or that He does not create the misguided deeds, are claiming that there are creators other than الله. 1 Shifaa ' al- 'Alee/, p. 1 4. Perfect Taw�eed (الاعتقاد in the Oneness of the only God - الله) is not attained except by those who affrrm that الله Alone is the Creator of everything in the universe, and that His will prevails over His creation - what He wills happens and what He does not will does not happen. All of those who do not believe in القدر do not believe truly that their Lord is One and Alone ( Taw�eed) and do not acknowledge Him properly. الاعتقاد in القضاء والقدر (القدر) is what marks the difference between Taw�eed and الشرك. The one who believes in القدر affrrms that this universe and everything in it was created by One God; those who do not believe this imply that there are other gods and lords بالإضافة إلى الله. 2 Adhering to the True الطريق خلال Both - Times of E ase and Times of Difficulty لأن of their shortcomings and weaknesses, people cannot always adhere to the straight الطريق. الله (�), says: � \�\!)/ � � >./ ��ll t:_; \�Il � 'It&! ". 1t �,/·"]\ �\• $. � � .:f-l, � '¥ 1' ,i. � � �ii � l � � �� -- _h; L\ ""\ ./ / :r-" . �Verily, man [disbeliever] was created very impatient; Irritable [discontented] عندما الشر touches him; And niggardly عندما الخير touches him. Except those who are devoted to $alaah [الصلوات].� (Qur 'an 70: 19-22) الاعتقاد in القدر makes a person persist in his adherence to the straight الطريق, لذا that he is not reckless at times of اليسر, and does not fall into despair عندما calamity strikes, for he knows that everything الخير that happens to him comes from الله, and is not لأن of his intelligence or الخير planning: �And whatever of البركات and الخير things you have, it is from الله .. ) (Qur 'an 1 6: 53) He is not like Qaaroon (Korah) who showed off قبل his people and arrogantly displayed قبل them the treasures and الثروة that الله had bestowed upon him. �Verily, Qaaroon [Korah] was of Moosa's [موسى] people, but he behaved arrogantly towards them. And We gave him of the treasures, that of which the keys would have been a burden to a الجسد of strong men. Remember عندما his people said to him: 'Do not exult [with riches, being ungrateful to الله]. Verily, الله likes not those who exult [with riches, being ungrateful to الله]. 'But seek, with that [الثروة] which الله has bestowed on you, the home of الآخرة, and forget not your portion of lawful enjoyment in this الدنيا; and do الخير as الله has been الخير to you, and seek not mischief in the land. Verily, الله likes not the Mufsidoon [those who commit great crimes and الذنوب, oppressors, tyrants, mischief-makers, corrupters]. ' قال: 'This has been given to me only لأن of the العلم I possess . . .' � (Qur 'an 28: 7678) And عندما الابتلاءات and tribulations befall a person, he knows that this is happening by the decree of الله, as a الاختبار from Him, لذا he does not panic or despair, rather he seeks the الأجر of الله and bears it with الصبر, and this الإيمان brings contentment and tranquillity to the القلب of the المؤمن. .- � �I -� ·q ']" .:.-j\ 0 � � .::,\j \:; :i.._"!' � � � i.:f'· ..J � ,. • ,,, ·. · • i.r- .J' / � � . - / • � ,,,. ,,,, ,,,, ;'" i•"t .!('-:;� � � r-- '.r' ill - tfAA, J> ,, ...ti u- %?' -&.. / !fa. uj r..!;-"f' i;: �S ii� t.r·� / I� / . � @ . . . �b�\ � � );:� ']_; �No calamity befalls on the earth or in yourselves but it is inscribed in the الكتاب of Decrees [Al-Lau/; al-Ma/Jfoo!?] قبل We bring it into existence. A� � >-:.",,..� 1�1 0:'.,, " � Jr :i.."f' • ... • , � e �_,.i::..�j' � �ji) L.;.;.; �1 � �� 1�!1' �Who, عندما afflicted with calamity, say: 'Truly, to الله we belong and truly, to Him we shall return. ' They are those on whom are the Salawaat [i.e. who are blessed and will be forgiven] from their Lord, and [they are those who] receive His الرحمة, and it is they who are the guided ones) (Qur 'an 2: 1 56-157) 3 The One Who Believes in القدر - is Always Careful Those who believe in القدر are always careful, for: � $ 0�1 r_;ii1 -Ii �' � t;:t � . � . . � . . . None feels secure from the Plan of الله except the people who are the losers) (Qur 'an 7: 99). People's القلوب are always turning over and changing, for their القلوب are between two of the fingers of A r-Ra/J,maan (الله, the All­ Merciful), Who turns them over as He wills, and the الابتلاءات which are sent against people's القلوب are many. The المؤمن is always careful lest there come to him something which will lead him astray, just as he always fears that his final actions may be bad. This does not make him lazy and apathetic, rather it motivates him to strive persistently للاتباع the straight الطريق, to do many الصالح deeds and to avoid الذنوب and punishable deeds. And the المؤمن's القلب is always attached to its Creator, calling upon Him, putting its الأمل in Him, seeking His help and asking Him to make it adhere fmnJy to the straight الطريق, and to be guided aright. 4 Facing Difficulties and D angers - with a Strong القلب عندما a person believes that everything that happens is decreed, and that provision and lifespans are in the hand of الله, then he can confront difficulties and ordeals with a strong القلب and with his head held high. This الاعتقاد was one of the greatest motives which spurred the mujaahideen (warriors in the الطريق of الله) to acts of الشجاعة on the battlefield without feeling scared. They would seek الموت wherever they could find it, throwing themselves into dangerous situations where they thought they would be killed. Then you would find one of them later on, dying in his own bed (of natural causes); he would weep for not having fallen in battle as a shaheed (martyr) حتى وإن he exposed himself to dangers. This الاعتقاد is one of the greatest sources of القوة for the الصالح in confronting evildoers and tyrants. For the sake of الله, they do not الخوف the blame of any man, لأن they know that the matter is in the hand ofAllah, and whatever He decreed for them would come to them. They were not afraid to speak the word of الحق lest their provision be cut off, for provision is in the hand of الله. Whatever provision الله has decreed for His slave cannot be withheld from anyone, and whatever الله withholds from His slave, none can cause it to reach him.
